findings.evidence: `Kernel.lean:71` defines `IsPositiveDefiniteKernel` for `[RCLike 𝕜]`; `FunctionKernel.lean:79-87` provides the function-to-kernel bridge; `Limits.lean:53-64` proves the same closed-under-pointwise-limits argument only for `M → ℂ`.
findings.fix: Add a general theorem such as `isPositiveDefiniteKernel_of_tendsto {𝕜} [RCLike 𝕜] {K : ι → α → α → 𝕜} {L : α → α → 𝕜} (hK : ∀ᶠ i in l, IsPositiveDefiniteKernel (K i)) (hlim : ∀ a b, Tendsto (fun i => K i a b) l (𝓝 (L a b))) : IsPositiveDefiniteKernel L`, then derive this function theorem via `IsPositiveDefinite.isPositiveDefiniteKernel`/`of_isPositiveDefiniteKernel`.
findings.issue: `of_tendsto` is under-generalized: it hard-codes `IsPositiveDefinite (M → ℂ)` and redoes the finite quadratic-form proof instead of using the existing kernel abstraction.

summary: The limit theorem is proved only for the function predicate even though the existing API already has a more general positive-definite kernel layer. Put the closure theorem at the kernel/RCLike level first, then derive the function statement from the bridge.
